item.title: WireGuard VPN developer can’t ship software updates after Microsoft locks account
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The popular open source VPN maker is the second high-profile developer to say Microsoft locked his account without notifying him and is blocking their ability to send software updates to users.

item.title: A self-driving car in Texas hit and killed a mother duck, sparking neighborhood outrage
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: An Avride autonomous vehicle near Austin hit the duck, facing backlash from the community. "It didn't slow down or hesitate at all, just steamrolled right through," according to a witness.

item.title: Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Poke brings AI agents to everyday users via text message by handling tasks and automations without complex setup, apps, or technical know-how.

item.title: Canva doubles down on AI and marketing automation with Simtheory, Ortto acquisitions
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Canva says the acquisitions add strengths in agentic AI, data infrastructure, marketing automation, and customer engagement.

item.title: AWS boss explains why investing billions in both Anthropic and OpenAI is an OK conflict
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: AWS has an ingrained culture of handling competition, he explained, because the cloud giant also competes with its partners.

item.title: Amazon to end support for older Kindle devices
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Amazon told Kindle owners this week that it's ending support for all e-readers released in 2012 or earlier, making them virtually unable to load any new content.

item.title: Meta debuts the Muse Spark model in a ‘ground-up overhaul’ of its AI
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: This is the first model Meta has released under its Superintelligence Labs, led by former Scale AI CEO Alexandr Wang.

item.title: Hack-for-hire group caught targeting Android devices and iCloud backups
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Security researchers exposed a spying campaign by a hack-for-hire group that used Android spyware and phishing to steal iCloud credentials and hack victims’ devices.

item.title: Tubi is the first streamer to launch a native app within ChatGPT
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Tubi becomes the first streaming service to offer an app integration within ChatGPT, the AI chatbot that millions of users turn to for answers.

item.title: Volkswagen begins testing its self-driving microbuses in Los Angeles ahead of launch with Uber
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Volkswagen subsidiary MOIA America still has a long and winding regulatory road before it can launch a commercial robotaxi service.

item.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

item.title: British cryptographer Adam Back denies NYT report that he is Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Back, a long-time researcher and pioneer in cryptography, denies the allegation that he is the secretive creator of Bitcoin.

item.title: Astropad’s Workbench reimagines remote desktop for AI agents, not IT support
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Astropad’s Workbench lets users remotely monitor and control AI agents on Mac Minis from iPhone or iPad, with low-latency streaming and mobile access.

item.title: Hackers steal and leak sensitive LAPD police documents
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: The LAPD said the breach affected “a digital storage system” belonging to the city’s Attorney's Office. The World Leaks extortion gang was reported to be behind the attack.

item.title: OpenAI releases a new safety blueprint to address the rise in child sexual exploitation
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: OpenAI's new Child Safety Blueprint aims to tackle the alarming rise in child sexual exploitation linked to advancements in AI.

item.title: Developer of VeraCrypt encryption software says Windows users may face boot-up issues after Microsoft locked his account
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The maker of the popular open source file encryption software VeraCrypt said Microsoft locked his online account, which may prevent device owners from booting up their computers.

item.title: Databricks co-founder wins prestigious ACM award, says ‘AGI is here already’
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Matei Zaharia has won the top honor from the Association for Computing Machinery. Now he's working on AI for research and says AGI is simply misunderstood.

item.title: As YouTube grows on TV, it eyes more interactive video across formats
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: YouTube's hiring across roles, partnerships, and a Bengaluru hub shows how it is building out its TV strategy.

item.title: Final 3 days to save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass until April 10, 11:59 p.m. PT. Secure your spot at the center of the tech ecosystem. Register today.

item.title: Atlassian launches visual AI tools and third-party agents in Confluence
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: Confluence users can now create visual assets within the software in addition to new third-party agents working with Lovable, Replit, and Gamma.
